metadata.subject: Mathematics
metadata.description: Master statistical methods for collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data to answer real-world questions. Learn how probability distributions underpin statistical inference and hypothesis testing. Investigate regression models for predicting relationships between variables. Apply statistical thinking to evaluate claims, understand study design, and make informed decisions based on evidence.
metadata.general_description: Develop statistical literacy through data analysis, probability, inference, and evidence-based decision-making.
cross_curricular_connections: Science - Experimental Design and Data Analysis
Health Sciences - Clinical Trials and Epidemiology

big_ideas.statement: Statistical thinking provides methods to draw reliable conclusions from data and quantify uncertainty in those conclusions.
big_ideas.elaboration: Statistics transforms raw data into meaningful information. Understanding variation and using probability allows us to make defensible claims about populations based on samples.

big_ideas.statement: How data is collected fundamentally affects what conclusions can be drawn; study design determines validity.
big_ideas.elaboration: Random sampling, randomization in experiments, and careful control of confounding variables ensure that statistical conclusions are trustworthy. Poor design leads to unreliable or misleading results.

big_ideas.statement: Probability distributions describe patterns of variation and provide the foundation for inferential statistics.
big_ideas.elaboration: Normal, binomial, and other distributions model real-world phenomena. Understanding these distributions enables us to predict likely outcomes and test hypotheses.

big_ideas.statement: Regression models quantify relationships between variables and enable predictions, but correlation does not imply causation.
big_ideas.elaboration: Linear and non-linear regression provide tools for describing how variables relate. However, demonstrating a relationship is not the same as proving that one variable causes changes in another.

categories.name: Reasoning and Modelling
categories.raw: Develop statistical reasoning through design of studies, selection of appropriate methods, and analysis of data.

competencies.raw: Design data collection methods and select appropriate statistical approaches based on research questions.
i_can_statements: I can identify population, sample, and variables in statistical contexts.
I can distinguish between observational studies and experiments and recognize their different limitations.
I can design sampling strategies and explain how randomness reduces bias.
I can identify sources of bias and confounding variables in studies and explain their effects.

categories.name: Understanding and Solving
categories.raw: Understand statistical concepts and methods by applying them to analyze data and test hypotheses.

competencies.raw: Calculate descriptive statistics, work with probability distributions, and conduct hypothesis tests to draw conclusions.
i_can_statements: I can calculate and interpret measures of center, spread, and position for distributions.
I can use probability distributions to calculate probabilities and make predictions.
I can conduct hypothesis tests and interpret p-values and confidence intervals correctly.
I can create and interpret regression models and use them to make predictions with appropriate caution.

categories.name: Communicating and Representing
categories.raw: Express statistical findings through appropriate graphs, tables, and verbal explanations with proper interpretation.

competencies.raw: Represent data visually and communicate statistical findings with clarity and appropriate context.
i_can_statements: I can create and interpret histograms, box plots, scatter plots, and other statistical graphs.
I can describe distributions using shape, center, and spread.
I can present statistical results with proper limitations and appropriate language about uncertainty.
I can critique graphs and statistical claims for accuracy, bias, and appropriate representation.

categories.name: Connecting and Reflecting
categories.raw: Connect statistical concepts across contexts and reflect on limitations and ethical implications of statistical methods.

competencies.raw: Reflect on how statistical thinking applies across disciplines and consider ethical dimensions of data collection and analysis.
i_can_statements: I can explain why understanding statistics is essential in a data-driven world.
I can identify misuses of statistics in media and marketing and explain what makes them misleading.
I can discuss ethical considerations in data collection and the importance of informed consent and privacy.
I can apply statistical thinking to evaluate claims and make informed decisions in personal and professional contexts.

areas.name: Data Collection and Study Design
areas.raw: Master methods for collecting reliable data and designing studies that support valid conclusions.

areas.name: Descriptive Statistics
areas.raw: Summarize and visualize data using statistics and graphs that reveal patterns and outliers.
topics: Measures of center: mean, median, mode
Measures of spread: range, IQR, standard deviation
Measures of position: percentiles, quartiles, z-scores

areas.name: Probability and Distributions
areas.raw: Understand probability theory and how probability distributions model real-world variation.
topics: Basic probability rules and conditional probability
Normal distribution and standard normal distribution
Using technology to work with distributions

areas.name: Inferential Statistics and Hypothesis Testing
areas.raw: Draw conclusions about populations based on samples using confidence intervals and hypothesis tests.
topics: Confidence intervals for means and proportions
Type I and Type II errors

areas.name: Regression and Correlation
areas.raw: Analyze relationships between variables using regression models and interpret associations appropriately.
topics: Predictions and limitations of regression models
